message:
feat(vdbe): collation-aware UNIQUE constraints in MemDatabase + preformatted Insert record path

VDBE-side complement to the core collation-aware UNIQUE constraint
fix in this same batch (see "fix(core): propagate collation through
UNIQUE constraint machinery").  This commit brings the MemDatabase
runtime enforcement path and the VDBE Insert opcode's record
handling into alignment with the new constraint metadata.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/engine.rs  (~478 line delta)

=== UniqueConstraintState: per-constraint collation tracking ===

- New struct UniqueConstraintState {
      columns: Vec<usize>,
      collations: Vec<Option<String>>,
      index: Option<BTreeMap<Vec<u8>, BTreeSet<i64>>>,
  }
- UniqueConstraintState::new(columns, mut collations) pads the
  collations vec to match columns.len() (resize with None when
  short, truncate when long) so later lookups never panic on
  index mismatch.  The optional BTreeMap is only populated when
  unique_constraint_supports_index(&collations) returns true —
  builtin collations (BINARY, NOCASE, RTRIM) can be normalized
  into canonical byte keys and indexed; non-builtin collations
  fall back to a row scan through the shared collation registry.
- New helper unique_constraint_collation_is_indexable(collation)
  returns true for None, BINARY, NOCASE, RTRIM and false
  otherwise.  This is the single decision point for "can we build
  a canonical byte index for this constraint?".

=== MemTable: restructure around UniqueConstraintState ===

- Replace the old
      unique_column_groups: Vec<Vec<usize>>
      unique_indexes: Vec<BTreeMap<Vec<SqliteValue>, BTreeSet<i64>>>
  with a single
      unique_constraints: Vec<UniqueConstraintState>
  plus a
      collation_registry: Arc<Mutex<CollationRegistry>>
  consulted when a constraint uses a non-builtin collation that
  cannot be turned into a canonical byte key.  Constructor
  initializes the registry to a fresh CollationRegistry so tables
  created without an attached engine still behave correctly.

- New MemTable::set_collation_registry(registry) replaces the
  shared registry in place; called from MemDatabase and
  VdbeEngine whenever either side rebinds its registry.

- add_unique_column_group(cols) now forwards to
  add_unique_column_group_with_collations(cols, Vec::new()).  The
  with_collations variant is the real work: it constructs a
  UniqueConstraintState, eagerly seeds the optional byte-keyed
  BTreeMap from the table's current rows (skipping NULL-bearing
  keys per SQLite's "NULL is distinct" rule), and pushes it onto
  unique_constraints.

- find_unique_conflicts(new_values) now iterates
  unique_constraints.  For constraints that have an index, the
  old get-by-key path is preserved (but keyed by Vec<u8>
  instead of Vec<SqliteValue>).  For constraints without an
  index (non-builtin collation), lazy-lock the shared collation
  registry once and fall back to a full row scan using
  unique_constraint_matches_row.

- clear() walks unique_constraints and clears only those that
  have an index; registry-backed constraints have no state to
  clear.

- New private helpers:
    unique_key_for_constraint(values, columns, collations)
      -> Option<Vec<u8>> — serialize a constraint key using
      append_unique_constraint_key_component for each column, or
      return None if any value is NULL.
    unique_constraint_matches_row(existing, new, columns,
      collations, registry) — per-column equality check via
      cmp_sqlite_values_collated looked up against the shared
      registry.
    append_unique_constraint_key_component — the canonical byte
      encoding for builtin collations:
        BINARY/None  → type-tag + raw bytes
        NOCASE       → type-tag + ascii-lowered bytes
        RTRIM        → type-tag + right-trimmed bytes
      Non-builtin collations return false so the caller falls
      back to the registry path.

=== MemDatabase: thread registry down to tables ===

- New MemDatabase::set_collation_registry(registry) that walks
  every table and calls set_collation_registry on it with a
  cloned Arc.  Called from VdbeEngine::bind_frontend_state when
  the registry Arc itself is swapped (collation_registry_changed
  path), and from VdbeEngine::set_database() whenever a fresh
  MemDatabase is attached — previously the new database would
  inherit an empty registry and every non-builtin collation would
  fall through to the "assume BINARY" fast path.

=== VdbeEngine: pipeline profiling integration + database attach ===

- Import enter_vdbe_decode_profile_stage and
  enter_vdbe_execute_profile_stage from the parent module (added
  in the sibling "vdbe: pipeline stage profiling markers" commit).
- run()/exec() opens a `_pipeline_profile_stage =
  enter_vdbe_execute_profile_stage()` RAII marker at the top so
  its whole body is scoped inside the execute stage timer.  The
  decode and commit stage entries are placed at the record
  decode and the Commit opcode sites respectively.
- set_collation_registry(): clone the Arc before storing so the
  db can be handed its own Arc::clone independently, then call
  self.db.set_collation_registry(registry) when a MemDatabase is
  attached.
- set_database(mut db): rebind the db's registry to match the
  engine's current registry before take ownership, so a freshly
  attached database starts correctly regardless of what registry
  it was constructed with.

=== VDBE Insert opcode: preformatted record fast path ===

The Insert opcode now recognizes a P4 operand of P4::Blob(bytes)
as a *preformatted* SQLite record and uses it verbatim instead of
re-serializing from the record register.  This is the runtime
side of the codegen change below.

- preformatted_record: Option<&[u8]> captures bytes directly from
  P4::Blob at the top of the Insert case.
- sideband_active is now `preformatted_record.is_none() &&
  lookaside.sideband_is_armed_for(record_reg)` so the sideband
  fast path is only considered when there's no explicit
  preformatted record.
- record_val materialization has three branches:
    preformatted_record.is_some()  → clear register subtype, set
        reg to SqliteValue::Null, record_val = Null.  The actual
        bytes will be taken from P4 further down.
    sideband_active                → existing fast path.
    else                            → existing take_reg().
- The `blob` selection when calling the storage backend is:
    if let Some(record) = preformatted_record { record }
    else if sideband_active { &sideband_buf[..] }
    else { record_blob_bytes(&record_val) }
- The MemDatabase fallback path (Phase 4 in-memory cursors) also
  routes through preformatted_record: when present, parse_record()
  is called once to materialize Vec<SqliteValue> for the in-memory
  insert, with metrics collected when collect_vdbe_metrics is on.

crates/fsqlite-vdbe/src/codegen.rs

- codegen_insert_values() now captures the return value of
  emit_table_insert_record() into `insert_p4` and forwards that
  as the P4 operand when emitting the Insert opcode, instead of
  unconditionally passing `P4::Table(table.name.clone())`.  This
  is what lets the codegen path opt into the preformatted-record
  fast path above — when the emitter can pre-assemble the record
  bytes itself (e.g. for VALUES lists of constants), it returns
  a P4::Blob instead of P4::Table, and the Insert opcode then
  uses those bytes verbatim at runtime.
- Both the "conflict path" and the "DO NOTHING" path are updated
  to use `insert_p4`.  No other call sites of
  emit_table_insert_record existed so this is a complete
  migration.

Tests:
- New MemTable unit tests assert that NOCASE-collated columns
  correctly detect 'Foo' vs 'foo' as conflicting, that RTRIM
  collated columns treat 'bar' and 'bar   ' as conflicting, and
  that a BINARY-collated column still treats them as distinct.
- New MemDatabase test verifies set_collation_registry propagates
  to every table.
- New VdbeEngine test exercises the full path: attach a registry,
  set_database, run an INSERT into a table with a NOCASE-UNIQUE
  column, then INSERT a conflicting casing — asserts the second
  insert raises the expected UNIQUE violation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
